figures.caption: Figure 3: Multi-stage data generation pipeline. Raw videos are converted into streaming training data through five stages: taxonomy balancing, type-consistent question construction, segmentation with relevance annotation, sliding-window pruning under partial observations, and conversation standardization.
Table 11: Prompt template used for task-aware full-video question generation in self-evolved data construction.
Table 12: Prompt template used to annotate whether each short segment contains visual evidence for each generated question.
Table 13: Prompt template used to select a question with sufficient segment-level evidence for causal roll-out.
Table 14: Prompt template used to convert accumulated segment evidence into a SILENT or response action.
Table 21: Prompt template used to score REC count consistency in Forward Active Responding.
Table 19: Prompt template used to score CRR responses in Forward Active Responding.
Table 20: Prompt template used to score SSR stage consistency in Forward Active Responding.

Table 8: Manual audit of n = 50 self-generated trajectories (base model: Qwen2-VL). Two authors independently labelled each trajectory and resolved disagreements by discussion. The fourth column states the pipeline mechanism each criterion relies on or that bounds its failure mode; the third column reports the measured pass rate.

Table 1: Qualitative comparison of streaming video adaptation methods. EvoStreaming requires no architectural modification or external inference module, uses 139× less supervision than the leading data-driven baseline, needs no human annotation, and generalizes across five diverse backbones without per-model engineering.
Figure 6: Dataset distribution overview. Left: task distribution; Right: video duration distribution.

Table 9: Token-level analysis of streaming behavior. We report the Per-Token Score (η), a proxy for the information density of each model under RealStreamEval. Lower η often reflects verbose or redundant outputs during timesteps where a streaming assistant should remain silent.
Table 16: Prompt template used to detect repetitive responses for the RealStreamEval repetition penalty.
Table 18: Prompt template used to detect descriptive or recognition intent for SSR and REC tasks.
Table 17: Prompt template used to determine whether a CRR output contains a substantive response intention.

Figure 4: Streaming performance analysis. Left: EvoStreaming improves overall OVOBench performance across base models. Right: EvoStreaming improves Forward Active Responding, i.e., deciding when proactive responses are needed.

Figure 2: Overview of RealStreamEval. We first align questions to sampled frames, then run strict online inference where the model decides when to respond, and finally score responses with a verbosity penalty to discourage redundant outputs.
Table 4: Implementation hyperparameters used for EvoStreaming. Settings are shared across all five base backbones unless otherwise noted.
Figure 8: A case of Backward Tracing. The model maintains silence while observing, then accurately retrieves information from memory when queried about past events.

Figure 7: A case of Forward Active Responding. The model remains silent during most frames and generates detailed descriptions only when the person transitions to a new step in the soy milk preparation process.
Figure 9: A case of Real-Time Visual Perception. The model provides immediate answers to visual queries about the current frame while remaining silent at other times.
Table 15: Prompt template used to judge answer correctness for Real-Time Visual Perception and Backward Tracing tasks.

Table 10: Task categories used by the self-evolved data-generation prompts. Each category induces a different type of streaming supervision, ranging from immediate visual grounding to patient monitoring and proactive response.

Figure 5: EvoStreaming reduces verbosity while improving accuracy. (a) Average tokens per turn for three Qwen-family backbones, with 6×–28× fewer tokens. (b) Accuracy gains per backbone from vanilla to EvoStreaming. Detailed numbers are in Appendix Table 9.
